one_line: A Bengal Army sepoy whose attack at Barrackpore in 1857 became a flashpoint before the great uprising.

appearance.age_look: Mid-20s, lean but wiry build, around 5'8" with a soldier’s upright posture.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown north Indian complexion, straight nose, strong jaw, faint stubble along chin and jawline, dark expressive eyes slightly narrowed with resolve; default expression is intense and serious, as if on the edge of action.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair tied back into a small, neat low bun at the back of the head (practical for a sepoy), with short loose strands at the temples; he wears no helmet in default look but sometimes a simple red cotton band around the forehead pushed slightly up like a sweatband, hinting at a tied turban removed for drill.
appearance.costume: Mid-19th-century Bengal Native Infantry uniform: a long, off-white cotton kurta-tunic reaching mid-thigh with a narrow standing collar and subtle side slits, worn under a vivid red woollen coatee cut to just below the waist with brass buttons down the front, plain shoulder straps without heavy epaulettes, and simple dark blue facings at the cuffs; straight, slightly loose white cotton churidar trousers tightly gathered at the calves, tucked into ankle-high brown leather boots with simple stitching; a broad dark blue cloth waist-belt/sash wraps once around the midsection, tying slightly to the left, and a narrow leather cartridge belt crosses diagonally from right shoulder to left hip; minimal jewellery, only a small plain iron kada bracelet on his right wrist and a thin black thread amulet under the collar; fabrics look slightly worn and sweat-darkened at seams, grounding the uniform in the dust and heat of Barrackpore.
appearance.signature: A period-accurate East India Company pattern flintlock musket (Brown Bess style) with a long dark-wood stock, brass fittings, iron barrel and bayonet lug, usually carried in his right hand with the long triangular bayonet fixed, the musket’s leather sling slung over his shoulder.

ref_desc: Mangal Pandey is a lean mid-20s sepoy with an oval medium-brown face, dark intense eyes, black hair in a low bun with loose temples, wearing an off-white kurta under a red Bengal Infantry coatee, white churidar trousers, dark blue sash, brown boots, and carrying a long Brown Bess-style flintlock musket with fixed bayonet in a palette of deep red, off-white, dark blue, brown leather, and brass.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character turnaround of a mid-19th-century Indian sepoy: a lean mid-20s man with an oval medium-brown face, strong jaw, faint stubble, and dark intense eyes, his thick black hair tied into a small low bun with a few loose strands at the temples and no helmet, suggesting practical drill readiness. He wears an off-white cotton kurta-tunic under a vivid red woollen Bengal Native Infantry coatee that ends just below the waist, with a narrow standing collar, brass buttons down the front, plain shoulder straps, and dark blue facings at the cuffs. His straight white cotton churidar trousers gather tightly at the calves and tuck into ankle-high brown leather boots with simple stitching. A broad dark blue cloth waist-sash wraps once around his midsection and knots slightly off-centre, while a narrow brown leather cartridge belt crosses from right shoulder to left hip. Show a small plain iron bracelet on his right wrist and a thin black thread amulet just visible at the neck, with the uniform slightly worn and sweat-darkened at seams to suggest use in the dusty heat. In his hands and slung with a leather strap, show a detailed East India Company flintlock musket (Brown Bess style) with long dark-wood stock, brass fittings, iron barrel, and a fixed triangular bayonet. Present him from front, side, and back in one image, keeping the consistent palette of deep red, off-white, dark blue, brown leather, and brass, and maintain a serious, resolute expression suitable for a historically grounded 1857 Barrackpore setting in dynamic shonen anime style.
